t_ch: 我看看这个顺序能不能对得上…嗯…罗刹从星槎上下来，进入长乐天，走进一间小客栈放下行李，甚至放下了他的宝贝棺材。
t_en: Lemme see if this order works... Hmm, Luocha exits the starskiff, enters Exalting Sanctum, goes into an inn, and puts down his luggage — including the coffin.

t_ch: 然后他去了三余书肆，没有买书。离开书肆之后，就钻进了一个小角落。
t_en: Then he goes to Spare Time Book Shop, but doesn't buy anything. Finally, he leaves and turns a corner into a dark alley.

t_ch: 逻辑很顺畅，看起来这就是正确的顺序了。{NICKNAME}确实很聪明。
t_en: The logic in this is sound — looks like the correct order. Nice work, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不错不错，不愧是我神探三月七精挑细选的小助手！
t_en: Nice work indeed — you're sidekick to a genius detective after all!

t_ch: 二三一四
嗯…二三一四…罗刹从客栈中出来，去了三余书肆，然后钻进了一个小角落。再之后…拎着行李出现在码头上……
t_en: Hmm... 2, 3, 1, 4... Luocha emerges from the inn, goes to Spare Time Book Shop, turns into an alley, then appears at the dock with his luggage...

t_ch: 我懂了！他在三余书肆搞到了一张藏宝图，去那个小角落寻宝，获得了巨额财富以后，拎着行李衣锦还乡！
t_en: I know! He must have found a treasure map at Spare Time Book Shop. He went into the alley to dig it up and then went home with his newfound riches!

t_ch: 才怪啦…这个顺序感觉不太对，{NICKNAME}，你再试试别的顺序吧。
t_en: On second thought, nah... {NICKNAME}, this order feels awkward, try arranging it again.

t_ch: 我想再看一下视频。
t_en: I wanna see the videos again.

t_ch: 行啊。你要看哪一段呀？
t_en: Sure, which one do you want to watch?

t_ch: 看看影像一。
t_en: Watch footage one.

t_ch: 罗刹穿过街道，走进了一个奇怪的角落。那里看起来很危险，但他似乎满不在乎，只拿着一把剑就踱步走了进去。
t_en: Luocha crossed the street and turned into a strange corner. It looks dangerous there, but he didn't seem to care at all. He paced in with nothing but a sword in hand.

t_ch: 看看影像二。
t_en: Watch footage two.

t_ch: 罗刹从一家小客栈中走出来。身上没有携带什么繁重的行李。
t_en: Luocha walked out of a small inn without any heavy luggage.

t_ch: 看看影像三。
t_en: Watch footage three.

t_ch: 罗刹进入了三余书肆，不久后走了出来。他两手空空，大概什么也没有买。
t_en: Luocha walked into Spare Time Book Shop and stepped out after a while. He left with nothing in hand — perhaps he didn't buy anything.

t_ch: 看看影像四。
t_en: Watch footage four.

t_ch: 罗刹在长乐天的码头上眺望着风景，旁边立着一口棺材。可能是刚下星槎，也可能是在等星槎。走了一会儿，他将行李放在一边，靠在栏杆上观看码头下往来的星槎。
t_en: Luocha is looking into the distance at the dock of Exalting Sanctum with his coffin at his side. Maybe he just left a starskiff, maybe he is waiting for a new starskiff. He walked along the dock for a while and put aside his luggage, leaning on the railing and watching starskiffs passing by the dock.

t_ch: 还是不看了。
t_en: Not now.

t_ch: 影像中的这个小角落是什么地方啊？
t_en: Where does this corner lead to?

t_ch: 我看了一下地图，这片空地似乎有一扇门…你们看，穿过这扇门，后面有一个码头。他可能搭乘星槎离开了。
t_en: I checked the map and found a gate in this open area... Look, there's a small dock on the other side. He may have left on a starskiff.

t_ch: 为什么要从这么偏僻的小码头离开长乐天？这也太可疑了吧？
t_en: Why would he leave Exalting Sanctum via a secluded dock? That's so suspicious!

t_ch: 我想，罗刹先生应该不是这么离开的。瓦尔特先生说的那是鸢首戊字九百九十九号码头——神策府的码头，仅在外敌入侵时启用。
t_en: I don't think Mr. Luocha could've departed from there. The dock you're referring to is Yuanshou-Crag CMXCIX — it belongs to the Seat of Divine Foresight. It's only used during invasions.

t_ch: 因此，那扇门也是常年上锁的。据我所知，已经锁了几百年了，只在偶尔检查时才打开。
t_en: That's why that gate is almost always locked. As far as I know, it's been locked for centuries and only gets opened for occasional inspections.

t_ch: 关键问题大概是…他是何时出去的。至少从地图上看，这片空地只有一个出入口…净砚小姐，从留存的影像里看，那天这里还有其他人出入吗？
t_en: The key question is... when did he leave? There's only one gate in this area, at least on the map... Miss Jingyan, does the remaining footage show anyone else entering or leaving this place?

t_ch: 这应该能查到，不过要稍等一下，虽然大部分影像都丢失了，但终究是一整天的录像，要查看的东西很多，我需要确认一下……
t_en: I can find out, but you'll have to wait awhile. Most of the footage was lost, but at least there was a whole day of recording. There's a lot that needs checking and confirming first...

t_ch: 嗯，辛苦了。
t_en: Understood, thank you.

t_ch: 辛苦你了。
t_en: Thanks for your hard work.

t_ch: 辛苦你啦！我们会耐心等待的。
t_en: Thanks so much! We'll be waiting — patiently.

t_ch: 诸位特地赶来帮忙，才是最辛苦的。我会尽快处理。
t_en: Your assistance in this matter is what requires gratitude. I'll get you what you need as soon as possible.

t_ch: 什么时候能好？
t_en: When will you be done?

t_ch: 喂，不要催人家嘛！不好意思啦净砚小姐，这家伙有点急性子。不必急，我们会耐心等待的。
t_en: Hey, don't rush her! So sorry, Miss Jingyan, they can be a little impatient. Take your time, we can wait.

t_ch: 没关系，我理解。毕竟诸位也是百忙之中抽时间来帮忙的。我会尽快处理。
t_en: Not to worry, I understand. After all, you're taking time away from your own affairs to lend a helping hand. I'll get everything to you as soon as possible.

t_ch: 久等了。我已经查阅了那片空地前的全部影像…不过，说是「全部」，其实丢失了非常多。
t_en: Thanks for waiting. I checked all the footage we have of the open area... I say "all" — a lot of it was lost.

t_ch: 那丢失的部分还能修复吗？就像我们刚才做的那样。
t_en: Are the corrupted parts recoverable? Can we use the same methods again?

t_ch: 也许有一部分可以修复…未必能行，我得试试。当然了，这需要一段时间，我没办法立刻给您变出来。
t_en: For some of them, maybe... but I can't guarantee anything. I'll do my best, of course. It'll take more time — I can't hand them over just now.

t_ch: 辛苦你了。那么残存的影像中是否有值得关注的内容呢？
t_en: Thank you. Did you find anything in the remaining footage worth paying attention to?

t_ch: 嗯…只有这段影像里，有人从那扇拱门中出去，时间在罗刹进入空地的大约一个时辰后，但是并没有拍到罗刹……
t_en: Hmm... Only that someone left the area through that exit around two hours after Luocha's appearance here. Luocha's nowhere to be seen, though.

t_ch: 啊？这？这不是《渔公案》里面出现过的剧情吗？
t_en: Huh? I... Isn't this a scene from The Angler Mystery?

t_ch: 看侦探小说看傻了？
t_en: That novel's gone to your head...

t_ch: 哎呀，讨厌你！我说真的，《渔公案》里出现过一模一样的场景。
t_en: Shh — you're being annoying! I'm not kidding — this is straight out of the book.

t_ch: 你在说什么剧情？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 《渔公案》啊，就那本侦探小说。里面有一幕和这段影像一模一样。
t_en: The Angler Mystery — the novel! There's a scene that's identical to this footage.

t_ch: 您这么一说，还真是。
t_en: Now that you mention it...

t_ch: 我没太听懂你们在说什么。
t_en: I'm not sure I follow.

t_ch: 就是渔公的起源故事。他本来是丹鼎司的医士，后来惹上了神秘的「饮茶会」。「饮茶会」的两个黑衣人，就趁着他独自一人落单，给他灌下了毒药。
t_en: The Angler's origin story, of course. He's a healer from the Alchemy Commission who gets on the wrong side of a mysterious organization called the Tea Society. Two agents in dark clothing wait for him to be alone and then poison him.

t_ch: 这毒药有持明「蜕生」一般的功效，竟让渔公返老还童，变成了小孩子。从此以后，幼儿渔公，就一边追查「饮茶会」，一边破解各种奇案。
t_en: The drug has the same de-aging effect as the Vidyadhara's hatching rebirth — the Angler gets younger and turns back into a child. From there on out, the Angler pursues the Tea Society while solving all kinds of strange cases.

t_ch: 为什么一个茶话会这么凶狠…
t_en: Why would a "tea society" be so vicious...?

t_ch: 的确，感觉他们的茶可能和姬子的咖啡差不多……
t_en: Their tea must be stronger than Himeko's coffee...

t_ch: 「饮茶会」只是代号罢了，难道坏蛋会管自己叫「坏蛋会」吗？总要起个没那么恐怖的名字掩人耳目吧！
t_en: The Tea Society is just a code name, did you think they'd call themselves the Evil Society? They have to have a nice-sounding name to conceal themselves!

t_ch: 这个药听起来能治疗魔阴身…
t_en: A drug like that sounds like it would help people stricken with mara...

t_ch: 不能哦。按照书里的设定，这种药只能让人的身体返老还童，却不能处理人灵魂的损耗。换句话说，就是只「蜕」，没「生」。
t_en: It wouldn't. In the book, the drug only de-ages the body — it can't repair damage to the soul. In other words, it's like molting, not a true rebirth.

t_ch: 「蜕生」是持明的特性…人类也可以返老还童吗？
t_en: Hatching rebirths are specific to the Vidyadhara... Can humans also experience de-aging?

t_ch: 哎呀，小说！艺术创作嘛，谁知道是不是真的！反正写得很像那么回事。
t_en: Eh, it's just a novel — who knows if something like that could happen in reality. I thought of it as soon as I saw this footage, though.

t_ch: 写这本书的苏芳老师本身就是丹鼎司医助，在医学原理的设定上还是很严谨的。
t_en: Su Fang, the author, was a medical assistant in the Alchemy Commission. The medical principles in the novel are strictly grounded in reality.

t_ch: 瞧，丹鼎司的人都这么写，没准就是可能的。
t_en: True... If someone from the Alchemy Commission was suggesting it could be done, then there's always a possibility.

t_ch: 所以小三月的意思是，那两个黑衣人是「饮茶会」的成员，而那个小孩就是…返老还童的罗刹？
t_en: So, March, what you mean is, the two people in dark clothing are Tea Society agents, and the child is... a de-aged Luocha?

t_ch: 搞不好就是某一位《渔公案》的狂热书友做出了模仿犯罪啊。罗刹被缩小以后就跟着这两个人走了…案情越来越可怕了，没准还是宗绑架案！
t_en: *gasp* What if a crazy Angler Mystery fan decided to commit a copycat crime? Luocha gets turned into a child, then follows a mysterious duo in black... The plot thickens — this could be a kidnapping!

t_ch: 首先，这个孩子是黑发。罗刹是金发。
t_en: *sigh* The child in the footage has black hair. Luocha's hair is blonde.

t_ch: 年纪大了，头发掉色了。
t_en: Adult Luocha might look blonde, but maybe he's just going gray with age...

t_ch: 对啊，完全说得通。
t_en: Yeah! Makes sense to me.

t_ch: 是啊，这发色都不一样。
t_en: True, their hair color is completely different.

t_ch: 嗯…也可能染发了！
t_en: Hmm... Maybe he dyed his hair!

t_ch: 几位稍等…我才看出来，这孩子是三余书肆的小店长隐书啊。
t_en: Hold your horses, everyone... I recognize this child now. That's Yinshu — the young shopkeeper at Spare Time Book Shop.

t_ch: 很遗憾，小三月，没有「返老还童」，也没有「渔公」。
t_en: Too bad, March. No de-aging, no Angler.

t_ch: 而且那小孩子还是个女孩。
t_en: And the child is a girl.

t_ch: …呃，神探的前几次推理，肯定是错的，这符合预期！
t_en: ...Uh, a d—detective's initial conjectures are bound to be wrong! Totally expected!

t_ch: 还记得贝洛伯格的尤利安…
t_en: Remember Julian back in Belobog...?

t_ch: 不不，这和他绝对不是一回事。
t_en: No, no, this is completely different.

t_ch: 机巧鸟没拍到那两个黑衣人的清晰长相…我们先找隐书问问吧。也许她看到了什么。
t_en: The cycranes weren't able to get a clear look at the two people in dark clothing... Let's ask Yinshu, maybe she saw something.

t_ch: 我继续尝试修复丢失的影像，如果有什么新进展，会联系各位的。
t_en: I'll keep trying to recover the lost footage. I'll contact you if there's a breakthrough.

t_ch: 好嘞，出发！开始寻访证人！
t_en: Alright, let's go! Time to interview the witness!

t_ch: 那个小店长好像不在。我们去附近找找看吧。
t_en: I don't see the shopkeeper. Let's look for her in the area.

t_ch: 现在还没到闭店时间，她应该不会去太远的地方才对。你们两个去吧，我在这里等等，也许她一会儿就回来了。
t_en: Business hours aren't over yet, she should be nearby. You two go ahead, I'll wait here in case she comes back.

t_ch: 好嘞。咱们走吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Okay, let's go {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 净砚带几人走到了四方览镜前，开始调取影像资料。
t_en: Jingyan takes everyone to the Foursquare Mirror and begins to retrieve the footage.

t_ch: 店长临时有事不在，请稍后再来。
t_en: Away on temporary business, please come back later

t_ch: 看来隐书不在啊…她现在会在哪里呢？
t_en: Looks like Yinshu isn't here after all... Where could she be?

t_ch: 芸慎大哥，我有点急事。你先帮我看一下店，我马上回来。隐书
t_en: Hi Yunshen! I have an errand to run, please watch the store for me while I'm gone. Back soon — Yinshu

t_ch: 原来她请了人替她看店啊…但是这个看店的人在哪里？怎么像是也不在店里的样子？
t_en: So she asked someone to look after the shop for her... but where are they? Looks like they must have left too.

t_ch: 妖异！神秘！诡谲！仙舟志怪集大成之作《述异幽明录》震撼发售！
t_en: Demonic! Mysterious! Treacherous! The Xianzhou masterpiece, Records of the Supernatural, on sale now!

t_ch: 嗯？是志怪小说短篇集诶，感觉很适合睡前看…虽然看完可能会不敢上卫生间。
t_en: Huh? A supernatural tales anthology! Sounds like a good bedtime read... Might stop me daring to run to the bathroom at night though...

t_ch: 《渔公案》系列时隔四十年的最新作——《蜕生迷局》，火热发售中！
t_en: After <unbreak>40</unbreak> years, the latest edition to The Angler Mystery series — The Hatching Rebirth Conundrum! Selling out everywhere!

t_ch: 原来这个系列隔了这么多年又出新作了！这罗浮我真是来对了！等查完这个案子，我一定要马上买来读。苏芳老师太强了。
t_en: *gasp* A new addition after so many years! Looks like I made the right choice coming to the Luofu! As soon as we've cracked this case, I'm getting myself a copy — Su Fang is the greatest.

t_ch: 你们去找人吧，我留在这里等等看。仙舟上很多书籍似乎挺有意思的，我也趁此机会了解一下。
t_en: You two go ahead, I'll wait here. The Xianzhou has some interesting literature — I'd like to get better acquainted.

t_ch: 我们快去快回，不会让杨叔久等的。
t_en: We'll be back soon, don't get too comfortable!

t_ch: 这位小哥，你是不是在帮三余书肆的那个小店长看店啊？
t_en: Hey mister, are you manning the counter for the young shopkeeper of Spare Time Book Shop?

t_ch: 啊？小店长？你说隐书啊？是啊，怎么了？有事？
t_en: Huh? Young shopkeeper? You mean Yinshu? Yeah, I am. Something wrong?

t_ch: 呃…能不能告诉我们，那个小店长在哪里呀？
t_en: Um... Can you please tell us where she is?

t_ch: 我为啥要告诉你们？
t_en: Why should I?

t_ch: 嘿，你这人，我们和和气气来问路，你这是什么态度啊？
t_en: Because we're asking nicely? What's with the attitude?

t_ch: 要告诉你们也行，付我五百镝。
t_en: I can tell you... if you pay me <unbreak>500</unbreak> strales.

t_ch: 凭啥给你巡镝啊？什么跟什么啊？怎么就开始要钱了？
t_en: Pay you? What is this? You want payment for a simple favor?

t_ch: 呵呵。情报和信息是最值钱的，你懂不懂啊？
t_en: Hehe, come on. No one ever tell you that information and intelligence are the most valuable commodities?

t_ch: 这家伙看起来不太讲道理，咱们怎么办？
t_en: This guy doesn't seem like the negotiable type. What should we do?

t_ch: 我们没钱，可以付你一顿毒打。
t_en: We have no money, but we can pay you with a beatdown?

t_ch: 你知不知道我是什么来路？竟然还敢威胁我？
t_en: Oh, you dare threaten me? Do you know who I am?

t_ch: 那你知不知道我们是什么来路？
t_en: The question is, do you know who we are?

t_ch: 我们可是「饮茶会」的！
t_en: We're from the Tea Society!

t_ch: 你糊弄谁呢？这不是小说里的组织吗？
t_en: Oh, you mean like the one in that work of fiction? Who are you trying to kid?

t_ch: 呵呵，就是因为有我们这种现实原型，《渔公案》里面才会这么写啊。
t_en: Hah — and who do you think were the inspiration for the Tea Society? Us, that's who.

t_ch: 听着像在诓我，但这两个人确实不像善茬儿……
t_en: Sounds like a pack of lies... Still, they don't seem like pushovers...

t_ch: 我们可是列车组的！
t_en: We're the Express Crew!

t_ch: 列车组…我怎么没听说过，该不会是其他仙舟上的大佬吧……
t_en: The Express Crew...? Never heard of you. I mean, they couldn't be higher-ups from one of the other Xianzhou ships... could they?

t_ch: 完了，不会真遇到硬茬子了吧？嗯…好汉不吃眼前亏。
t_en: Dang it, am I in trouble? Ugh... Better to live and fight another day.

t_ch: 算了，不跟你们一般见识。隐书说，长乐天那边有家小吃摊的优惠券要到期了，赶着去用掉…她走了没多久，现在可能刚吃完吧。
t_en: *sigh* Forget it, I'm not stooping to your level. Yinshu said she had a voucher for a food stall over in Exalting Sanctum — she wanted to use it before it expired... You know, she's not been gone long — probably finishing up her food right now.

t_ch: 哼，算你识相。{NICKNAME}，我们走。
t_en: Hmph, about time you saw the light. Come on {NICKNAME}, let's go.

t_ch: 给你钱，可不许骗我们。
t_en: I'll pay you, but don't even think about lying.

t_ch: 你别掏钱。这是我神探三月七的案子，当然应该是我来掏钱了！
t_en: In your dreams! This is genius detective March 7th's case — I should be the one paying...

t_ch: 谁掏钱都一样，搞快点。
t_en: Yeah, yeah, yeah – same difference, pay up.

t_ch: 要是骗我们，我们会回来找你算账的！
t_en: If you lie to us, we'll be back!

t_ch: 呵呵，我可不是这种人。隐书说，长乐天那边有家小吃摊的优惠券要到期了，赶着去用掉…她走了没多久，现在可能刚吃完吧。
t_en: Hehe, I'm not that kind of person. Come on! Yinshu said she had a voucher for a food stall over in Exalting Sanctum — she wanted to use it before it expired... You know, she's not been gone long — probably finishing up her food right now.

t_ch: 我的巡镝啊！整整五百镝啊！本姑娘的心在滴血！{NICKNAME}，我们走。
t_en: <unbreak>500</unbreak> strales! <unbreak>500</unbreak>! Ugh, my purse is crying. Come on {NICKNAME}, let's go.

t_ch: 你就是三余书肆的小店长隐书吧？我们有些事要找你。
t_en: Are you Yinshu, the shopkeeper at Spare Time Book Shop? There's something we want to talk to you about.

t_ch: 嗯…我就是隐书。请问有什么事吗？
t_en: Yeah... that's me. Can I help you?

t_ch: 你现在正好要回书肆吧？那我们一起走吧，路上给你讲。
t_en: You're heading back to the store, right? Let's walk and talk. I'll tell you all about it on the way.

t_ch: 啊…好的，走吧。
t_en: Oh, uh... okay. Let's go.

t_ch: 你们一起回到三余书肆。在路上，你们给隐书讲述了前因后果。
t_en: You return to Spare Time Book Shop. On the way over, you get Yinshu up to speed.

t_ch: 你们的那段录像，我有些印象。那天书肆打烊之后，我想找个地方去安安静静地读书，就在街上转了几圈。
t_en: That footage... I remember that day. After I closed up the store, I walked around for a while. I was looking for a place to read.

t_ch: 然后我就发现了那个空地…不过我一看，那里面有两个黑衣人和一个金发化外民，觉得实在不是看书的地方，就走了。那两个黑衣人正好跟着我，也离开了。
t_en: I found that empty area — it seems like a good reading spot at first... but then I noticed two people dressed in dark clothing and a blonde outworlder hanging around. Something didn't feel right, so I left. Those two people left the same way I did.

t_ch: 原来只是恰好同路…那你有没有注意到那个金头发的化外民在做什么？
t_en: So you just happened to be going the same way... Did you see what the outworlder was doing?

t_ch: 实在不好意思，我当时只想找个地方踏踏实实读书，没去太仔细观察他，也没太仔细观察那两个黑衣人。
t_en: Sorry, I was only trying to find a place to read, I didn't pay much attention to him... or the two in dark clothing.

t_ch: 只记得…那两个黑衣人闻起来臭臭的。但这可能算不上什么线索啦。不好意思，你们专程跑一趟，但我没提供什么有价值的信息。
t_en: All I remember is... the two people in dark clothing — they smelled pretty bad. I guess that's not much of a clue. Sorry I can't give you any useful information.

t_ch: 不，这些都很有价值。非常感谢你。
t_en: On the contrary, any information you can give is valuable. Thank you very much.

t_ch: 不过，根据机巧鸟的影像，当天罗刹去那片空地前的最后一站，就是你们店里。
t_en: Still, according to the cycrane recordings, Luocha's final stop before heading towards the open area was your store.

t_ch: 我们店里…在三余书肆？他来过吗…哦，对了！我想起来了，他来过，我见过他的，怎么给忘了呢？
t_en: My store... Spare Time Book Shop? You're sure he came to... Oh, that's right! I remember now, he did pay a visit — how could I forget?

t_ch: 那天，他来到店里，翻了几本书之后，将一本书拿给我…那是一本纸书，很老派。
t_en: He came in, looked at a few titles, and then handed one to me... an old paperback.

t_ch: 我还以为他是要买书，没想到，他付了钱，然后撕掉了书的扉页。我吓了一跳，但看他满脸笑容，也没敢多问。
t_en: Everything seemed normal, but after paying for it he immediately tore off the title page. I was shocked, but he was grinning ear to ear, so I didn't dare ask him about it.

t_ch: 撕掉扉页以后，书他也没带走，放在柜台上人就离开了。
t_en: After that, he just left the book on the counter and went on his way.

t_ch: 这么奇怪的事情，我竟然忘得一干二净…可能是隔天建木生发，出现了太多惊人的事，让这件事都显得微不足道了吧……
t_en: I can't believe I'd forget something like that... I guess the Ambrosial Arbor's resurrection the day after pushed everything else to the back of my mind...

t_ch: 那么，他拿走的是哪本书呢？
t_en: So, what was the book?

t_ch: 是这本《渔公案》。书被撕掉了扉页，也不能放在店里卖了，我一直在想该怎么处理…既然你们问起来了，就送给你们吧。
t_en: The Angler Mystery. I was wondering what to do about it — I can't sell a book without a title page... But, since you asked about it, here — you can have it.

t_ch: 杨叔，你果然神机妙算啊。罗刹这家伙是真的坏，怎么会有这么坏的人…《渔公案》这么好的书，他说撕就撕。太气人了。
t_en: Mr. Yang's instinct was right all along — Luocha is a villain! How could he do this? Tearing up a book as well-written as The Angler Mystery... Unacceptable!

t_ch: 罗刹为什么撕书啊？
t_en: Why did Luocha tear out the title page?

t_ch: 我要是懂坏家伙怎么想的，岂不跟他一样坏了吗。
t_en: If I knew the answer to that, wouldn't that make me as evil as him?

t_ch: 是啊，这人也太坏了！
t_en: Right? Outrageous!

t_ch: 你们两个先冷静一下，我觉得罗刹…不像这么幼稚的人。
t_en: Calm down a moment, you two. I don't think Luocha has anything against the book.

t_ch: 小三月可能有点激动，忘记了地衡司最早关注的疑点——是否有人将「危险品」带入了罗浮。
t_en: Let's not get ahead of ourselves. We're forgetting that the Realm-Keeping Commission was initially investigating whether someone brought a "dangerous object" onto the Luofu.

t_ch: 大毫他们可能并不清楚这个「危险品」是什么，但我们都清楚，那就是「星核」。
t_en: Dahao and the others are probably not aware of what that "dangerous object" might be. But we know for a fact that it's the Stellaron.

t_ch: 所以，「撕书」这个行为，也许的确是问题的关键。
t_en: I think that by removing the page, Luocha may have provided us with a key piece of the puzzle.

t_ch: 我有点没跟上，撕书还和星核有关系？{NICKNAME}，你听明白了吗？
t_en: I don't understand. What does tearing out a page have to do with the Stellaron? Are you following Mr. Yang, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 明白了。
t_en: I think so.

t_ch: 罗刹撕书没有来由，必有深意。
t_en: It might seem like a random action at first glance, but there has to be more to it.

t_ch: 罗刹撕书是给同伙的接头暗号。
t_en: Maybe the page will mean something to his accomplices.

t_ch: 正是如此。也许罗刹在罗浮上有内应，而他和内应之间就通过书的扉页来相互联系。
t_en: Exactly. I think Luocha may have friends on the Luofu, and they're using the title page to communicate.

t_ch: 不明白…
t_en: I'm lost...

t_ch: 没关系，是我的思路有点跳跃。
t_en: That's alright, this is a bit of a conjectural leap.

t_ch: 我的意思是，也许罗刹在罗浮上有内应，而他和内应之间就通过书的扉页来相互联系。
t_en: What I'm trying to say is — I think Luocha may have friends on the Luofu, and they're using the title page to communicate.

t_ch: 那岂不是更坏了？竟然用《渔公案》这种歌颂正义的小说作为邪恶计划的接头暗号。
t_en: That makes him even worse! How dare he use a book that praises justice for his evil plans!

t_ch: 这只是猜测。我们目前没办法知道他做了什么…因为机巧鸟上的影像资料都丢失了。
t_en: I'm afraid "evil plans" are still within the realm of speculation at this stage. We have no way of knowing exactly what he did because the cycrane data was lost.

t_ch: 哎，那线索岂不是断掉了？
t_en: So... is this a dead end?

t_ch: 瞧，这真是天助我也！神探刚遇到死胡同，立刻就出现了新的线索。
t_en: Ha! That's more like it! New clues always turn up in the nick of time.

t_ch: 芸慎…那家伙果然不见了…我急着去用掉优惠券，才找来他看店。他平时在附近游手好闲，但是为人还不错…但果然还是不值得信任。
t_en: Where did Yunshen get to, I asked him to watch the shop while I used my voucher... He's often hanging around here — always seemed nice enough. *sigh* Not trustworthy after all.

t_ch: 他还想讹我们五百镝呢，纯纯的坏家伙。
t_en: That crook wanted to cheat us out of <unbreak>500</unbreak> strales.

t_ch: 啊？你们没给他吧？
t_en: What? You didn't give him the money, did you?

t_ch: 当然没有！我们三言两句就把他吓跑了。
t_en: Of course not! We scared him off.

t_ch: 两位英雄真是厉害。我之后也会找他算账的。哎…让这家伙来看店，是我的失职，真是太对不起了。
t_en: You two are incredible. I'll have to have a word with him later... Ugh, asking him to help was my mistake — I apologize.

t_ch: 没事啦！孰能无过呢。
t_en: No worries! You weren't to know.

t_ch: 是呀，犯错就是成长的一部分呢。
t_en: That's right. Making mistakes is part of growing up.

t_ch: 收下这个吧，就当做我的赔礼了，可好吃了。
t_en: Here — a little something for the inconvenience. It's yummy.

t_ch: 确实不太好，下次别这样了。
t_en: It was a bit of a goof, huh...

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我以后一定不会擅离职守了。
t_en: Mm-hmm, I won't be so quick to leave my post in the future.

t_ch: 收下这个吧，就当做我的赔礼了，可好吃了。
t_en: Here — a little something for the inconvenience. It's yummy.

t_ch: 不过，请不要把这件事告诉西衍先生…虽然平时不怎么出现，但他算是我们店的掌柜。要是被他知道了，我一定会挨骂的。
t_en: It'd be best if Mr. Xiyan didn't find out about this... He doesn't show up very often, but he's the manager here. I'll be in big trouble if he finds out what happened.

t_ch: 放心好啦，我们嘴巴可严了。但是下次还是不要这么做了哦。
t_en: Don't worry, we can keep a secret. Just make sure it doesn't happen again!

t_ch: 芸慎…那家伙果然不见了…我急着去用掉优惠券，才找来他看店。他平时在附近游手好闲，但是为人还不错…但果然还是不值得信任。
t_en: Where did Yunshen get to, I asked him to watch the shop while I used my voucher... He's often hanging around here — always seemed nice enough. *sigh* Not trustworthy after all.

t_ch: 他还讹了我们五百镝呢，纯纯的坏家伙。
t_en: That crook cheated us out of <unbreak>500</unbreak> strales.

t_ch: 真不好意思，这也是我的错。我之后会找他算账的。这五百镝，我来赔给你们吧。
t_en: What!? I apologize, this is my fault — I'll have a word with him later. Let me give you your money back first.

t_ch: 没必要，不是你的错。
t_en: No need, you couldn't have known this would happen.

t_ch: 就是就是。是那个家伙的问题。
t_en: Yeah, it's his problem, not yours.

t_ch: 哎…毕竟还是我擅离职守导致的。
t_en: *sigh* At the end of the day, this wouldn't have happened if I'd stayed at my post.

t_ch: 收下这个吧，就当做我的赔礼了，可好吃了。
t_en: Here — a little something for the inconvenience. It's yummy.

t_ch: 那我们就不客气了。
t_en: Thanks very much.

t_ch: 哎！你怎么就收下了！隐书，这不是你的错，你不用这样的。
t_en: Hey, you're gonna take it!? Yinshu, this isn't your fault — you don't have to do this.

t_ch: 哎…毕竟还是我擅离职守导致的。
t_en: *sigh* At the end of the day, this wouldn't have happened if I'd stayed at my post.

t_ch: 这个钱我们不能收，你辛苦看店，赚点钱也不容易。
t_en: This is your hard-earned money. Looking after a bookshop is no easy task.

t_ch: 那就收下这个吧，可好吃了。
t_en: Th—Thanks... In that case, have this instead — it's tasty.

t_ch: 你人真好！以后我会常来光顾这家店的。
t_en: Aw, that's so kind of you! You just made a loyal customer out of me.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，请一定常来。
t_en: Glad to hear that! Please come by anytime!

t_ch: 不过，请不要把这件事告诉西衍先生…虽然平时不怎么出现，但他算是我们店的掌柜。要是被他知道了，我一定会挨骂的。
t_en: It'd be best if Mr. Xiyan didn't find out about this... He doesn't show up very often, but he's the manager here. I'll be in big trouble if he finds out what happened.

t_ch: 放心好啦，我们嘴巴可严了。但是下次还是不要这么做了哦。
t_en: Don't worry, we can keep a secret. Just make sure it doesn't happen again!

t_ch: 各位，我们现在知道罗刹是什么时候离开那片空地的了。
t_en: So, we now know what time Luocha left the open area...

t_ch: 不错不错，是拍到了他离开的身影吗？
t_en: Nice! You found footage of him leaving?

t_ch: 正是如此。根据机巧鸟的记录，他在进入空地的一个时辰后，离开了那片空地。
t_en: Yes. According to the cycrane recordings, he left the area two hours after he entered.

t_ch: 怪了…那么一小块地，他在里面待了两小时？这个罗刹在干什么……
t_en: Strange... Why spend two hours in such a confined area? He must've been up to something...

t_ch: 他肚子不舒服？
t_en: Diarrhea?

t_ch: 停停停…你看他那穿着打扮和优雅举止，这种人做坏事很有可能，但不会不讲公德……
t_en: I'm gonna stop you right there. Refined wardrobe, elegant bearing... He may be a villain, but he doesn't look like someone who'd give up on finding a bathroom, even in an emergency...

t_ch: 他在睡午觉？
t_en: Afternoon nap?

t_ch: 会不会是旅费全部用来买星芋啵啵，没钱住旅店了？
t_en: Maybe he spent all his money on startaro bubble tea and couldn't afford a hotel room!

t_ch: 就连小三月都做不出这种事吧。
t_en: Oddly specific, March...

t_ch: 可惜那片空地里没有机巧鸟，我们还是不知道他究竟做了什么…但还真是，越查越可疑。
t_en: It's a shame no cycranes are installed in that area — we still have no idea what he was up to. This Luocha is getting more suspicious by the minute...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，咱们家杨叔从来不把情绪写在脸上，但我感觉他是不是有点…不安？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I know Mr. Yang never wears his heart on his sleeve, but do you get the feeling he's a little... restless?

t_ch: 应该不是不安，只是担忧。
t_en: I wouldn't call it restless. A little concerned, maybe.

t_ch: 哦，没事。我只是…对其它世界的「罗刹」有点固有印象，止不住有些担心。
t_en: Ha, don't worry about me. It's just... my mind keeps wandering back to those other "Luochas" I mentioned. Not the greatest memories.

t_ch: 他和长成这样的人恩怨很深吧…
t_en: I reckon he must have bad blood with the other "Luochas..." he mentioned.

t_ch: 和这个世界毫无关系。我只是有些担忧…担忧故乡的事在这里重演。
t_en: Remember, we're talking about different worlds here. However, I can't deny I'm a little worried that... what happened to my home world could befall this place too.

t_ch: 他耳朵怎么这么灵！
t_en: How does he always hear us!?

t_ch: 我还有些公务要处理，先失陪了。有什么事情随时联系我，各位都有我的联系方式。
t_en: Well, I'm afraid I have some other business to attend to. Let me know if you need anything — you know how to reach me.

t_ch: 好！辛苦你啦，净砚小姐。
t_en: Thanks for the help, Miss Jingyan!

t_ch: 所以在那「空白的两小时」中，罗刹究竟做了什么呢……
t_en: So... what did Luocha get up to during those missing two hours...?

t_ch: 看来是时候发挥我神探三月七的侦探能力了。
t_en: I think it's high time detective March took the gloves off.

t_ch: 小三月打算怎么做呢？要去现场看看吗？
t_en: Oh? And what do you have in mind, detective? Fieldwork?

t_ch: 杨叔，这你就不懂了。渔公说过：「对真正的神探来说，即使足不出户，也仿若亲临现场。」
t_en: That won't be necessary, Mr. Yang. As the Angler once said: "A true detective operates as effectively from their armchair as from the scene of the crime."

t_ch: 懒得跑图就直说。
t_en: Or to put it another way: "I'm too lazy to explore the map."

t_ch: 可不能现在去现场，要等到推理结束，到现场验证，你们发现真相竟和我说的分毫不差，这才是神探的浪漫！
t_en: It's too early to start fieldwork — we must wait until my deduction is complete. Then, when we arrive at the scene, you'll see that the facts match my theory to a T!

t_ch: 建议你向执事们道歉。
t_en: I think the Realm-Keeping officers might disagree with you there...

t_ch: 哎呀，「执事们亲临现场固然可敬，但终究欠缺了推理的灵光。」这也是渔公说的。
t_en: Hmph, I'll quote the Angler again: "The field officer is a diligent specimen, yet they lack the detective's instinct to deduce facts from evidence."

t_ch: 既然小三月兴致这么高，那就试试看吧。
t_en: Hehe, well, seeing as you're so confident, let's give your idea a try.

t_ch: 好耶，杨叔对我最好了！
t_en: Yay! Mr. Yang is the best!

t_ch: 我要开始还原真相咯，你准备好了吗？
t_en: Ready? I'm gonna start my reconstruction.

t_ch: 好了，开始吧。
t_en: I'm good. Let's do it.

t_ch: 好，那咱们开始吧…第一次当众推理，还真有点紧张。
t_en: Okay, here we go... Eh-heh, explaining a theory in front of everyone is a more nerve-wracking than I expected.

t_ch: 还没有，稍等一下。
t_en: Not yet, give me a second.

t_ch: 好，我也酝酿一下。
t_en: Sure, gives me time to make a few more adjustments.

t_ch: （好的，接下来我将采取渔公的推理方式：将自己代入坏蛋的视角中。）
t_en: (Right. I'll be using the Angler's deductive method: Reconstruct what happened from the bad guy's perspective.)

t_ch: 现在，本姑娘就是罗刹～
t_en: Aaaand now I'm Luocha~

t_ch: 不像在推理，倒像被夺舍。
t_en: Is this deduction or necromancy?

t_ch: 杨叔，我害怕。
t_en: Mr. Yang, I'm scared.

t_ch: （你别捣乱，我正在高速思考。）
t_en: (Quit messing around, I'm thinking.)

t_ch: （哦，对了，差点忘了这个撕掉的扉页…他把这张纸带走，那八成是有大用的……）
t_en: (Oh, almost forgot about that title page... He took it with him, so it must have had some significance.)

t_ch: 这东西…之后应该有用，最好先别扔掉。
t_en: You'd better keep it. This thing... may be useful in the future.

t_ch: （接下来…杨叔，借我看一下现场地图。嗯…嗯…他肯定穿过了前面的那扇门，不可能在这么小的地方晃悠了两小时。）
t_en: (Next up... I need to have a look at the map, Mr. Yang. Hmm... He must have gone through that gate up ahead. No way he would've stayed put in such a small area for two hours.)

t_ch: （可是净砚小姐也说了，那扇门是军队的资产，长年紧锁。）
t_en: (But Miss Jingyan mentioned that gate is a military asset — it's locked all year round.)

t_ch: （罗刹先生肯定有办法解开这个锁啊。连一扇门都打不开，还惦记偷运星核呢？）
t_en: (You think that would stop the likes of Luocha? What kind of a Stellaron smuggler would he be if he couldn't get past a door?)

t_ch: 哼，这种等级的门锁，还想拦住本姑娘？
t_en: Hmph, you think a lock like this can stop a girl like me!?

t_ch: 可以了，我受不了了。
t_en: I think I'm actually going insane.

t_ch: 你这样我真没法代入。
t_en: I'm losing the immersion...

t_ch: （咳咳，习惯了。好吧，接下来你们就想象罗刹那张脸会说什么台词，我不掺和。）
t_en: (*sigh* Fine, I'll be me. You guys will have to imagine his lines instead.)

t_ch: …这种档次的锁，能挡得住谁？
t_en: ...Such a rudimentary lock. Easy pickings.

t_ch: （我们先往那个方向走吧。从地图上来看，那里似乎很适合坏蛋做坏事。）
t_en: (Let's try that direction first. On paper, that area looks the perfect place for bad guys to do bad things.)

t_ch: （等一下，这条路太顺了。）
t_en: (Hold on... this would've been too quick a route.)

t_ch: 什么意思？顺还不好吗？
t_en: And? What's wrong with a quick route, hmm?

t_ch: （咳咳咳…顺还不好吗？）
t_en: (Ahem... What's wrong with a quick route, Mr. Yang?)

t_ch: （我们要试着解释罗刹在两小时之中做了什么。可是围着这个地方绕上二十圈，也用不了两个小时。）
t_en: (We're trying to uncover what Luocha did during those two hours. But even if he repeated this route twenty times, it wouldn't have taken him that long.)

t_ch: （确实啊…那他的路程可能比我想象中凶险很多啊。来吧，我们重新还原一次现场。）
t_en: (*sigh* True... He must've had a tougher journey than I imagined. Come on, let's start over.)

t_ch: 嗷嗷嗷！看你细皮嫩肉，竟敢擅闯这里，今天就是你的末日！
t_en: RAAAAWWWWWRRRRR! You look like a tasty morsel! Those who dare to enter here must face a delicious fate!

t_ch: 宵小之辈安敢放肆若此，着我一剑！
t_en: Your eyes are bigger than your stomach, foul villain! Have at you!

t_ch: （为什么怪物会说话？）
t_en: (Why is the monster talking now?)

t_ch: （为了增加一些紧迫感……）
t_en: (Y'know, just a little dramatic effect...)

t_ch: （我个人并不认为罗浮上会有这么危险的地方…这种会有危险分子和黑市买家接头的地方，至少在建木生发之前，应该是不存在的。）
t_en: (Monster lairs and black market spaces... I doubt the Luofu has any of that. At least, I doubt things of that nature existed before the Ambrosial Arbor's resurrection.)

t_ch: （都有坏蛋要在这里和星核的买家接头了……）
t_en: (Is it really so far-fetched? We're already assuming that Luocha came here to sell the Stellaron to a bunch of cut-throats — may as well throw in some monsters here and there...)

t_ch: 有必要一上来就这么刺激吗？
t_en: Does the story have to be this... stimulating?

t_ch: （这样比较有意思嘛。）
t_en: (It's more fun this way...?)

t_ch: 你这是侦探故事还是武侠？
t_en: Is this a detective story or an action movie?

t_ch: （渔公既是侦探，也是侠客！）
t_en: (The Angler is both a detective and a fighter!)

t_ch: （反正也很难想象罗刹具体遇到了什么困难，就当做是这样好了。）
t_en: (Anyway, it's not like we're gonna be able to conjure up exactly what Luocha ran into. Why not use our own interpretation to bridge the gap?)

t_ch: （嗯…勉强也算说得通。）
t_en: (Hmm... I suppose that makes some kind of sense.)

t_ch: 杨叔你也玩得很开心啊…
t_en: You're clearly enjoying this, Mr. Yang...

t_ch: （呃，既来之则安之……）
t_en: (I, uh... I try to go with the flow.)

t_ch: 到底哪里说得通了？！
t_en: Make sense how!?

t_ch: （没有什么明显的破绽。）
t_en: (I don't see any major plot holes.)

t_ch: 未料到此地如此凶险，看来往后行事，须得更稳妥些。
t_en: I didn't expect this place to be so treacherous. I'll need to be on my guard going forward.

t_ch: 不知那位对星核有意的神秘买家是何许人也……
t_en: I wonder, just who is the buyer interested in the Stellaron...

t_ch: 真难缠，这仙舟罗浮哪来这么多敌人？
t_en: Ugh, what are all these enemies doing on the Luofu!?

t_ch: 我还想问呢。
t_en: Same question.

t_ch: 三月七解释下。
t_en: March 7th, explain yourself.

t_ch: （这是因为…因为…因为……）
t_en: (It's becaaaause... because, because, because...)

t_ch: （…三月，你想剧情的时候我们可以等，不是非得安排战斗不可。）
t_en: (...March, you don't have to add in combat scenes while you're figuring out where to take the story — we can wait.)

t_ch: 站住。你是来做什么的？
t_en: Stop right there. State your business.

t_ch: 怎么是杨叔？
t_en: Is that Mr. Yang?

t_ch: （不，那是「饮茶会」的守门人「普洱」。）
t_en: (No, that's Pu'er, the Tea Society's gatekeeper.)

t_ch: （「普洱」不是茶的名字吗？）
t_en: (Isn't pu'er a type of tea?)

t_ch: （对啊，「饮茶会」的成员，名字当然是各种茶啦。）
t_en: (That's right. He's a member of the Tea Society, so, naturally, he chose a type of tea as his alias.)

t_ch: 问你小子话呢！你是来做什么的？
t_en: Hey, I'm talkin' to you! What's your business here?

t_ch: （你让他说话文明点…）
t_en: (Can you tell him to be less aggressive...?)

t_ch: （哦哦，好的。）
t_en: (Oh, sure.)

t_ch: 问您小子话呢！您是来做什么的？
t_en: Excuse me, sir! May I ask whether you're here on business, or...?

t_ch: （…算了，就这样吧。）
t_en: (...Nevermind.)

t_ch: 我来送一件东西，必须亲自交给你们老板，帮我联系一下他。
t_en: I have a delivery for your boss — I need to give it to him in person. If you could let him know I'm here.

t_ch: 交货？…啊，老大等您很久了，但您需要先证明身份，我才能让您见到老板。
t_en: Delivery? ...Ah yes, the delivery — the boss is waiting for you. I'm afraid I'll need to see some ID first.

t_ch: （这个时候，就轮到那个东西派上用场啦。）
t_en: (I wonder if Luocha has something prepared for this moment...)

t_ch: 「打感情牌」
t_en: Trying to persuade

t_ch: 您看，我上有老下有小，不卖掉这颗星核，全家今晚都得挨饿…您就通融一下，让我见见您家老大吧。
t_en: You see, I'm selling this Stellaron to bring food to my dear family — or we'll all have to starve tonight. So, be a good chap and do me a favor, will you?

t_ch: 卖星核的小罗刹？
t_en: So Luocha's an impoverished Stellaron peddler now?

t_ch: （这是罗刹的计谋，故意示弱，让对方麻痹大意。）
t_en: (This is Luocha's trick — feign weakness to get the gatekeeper to lower his guard.)

t_ch: 上有老下有小就别做坏事了…
t_en: If Luocha's such an upstanding family man, shouldn't he have considered not stealing the Stellaron in the first place?

t_ch: （就…生计所迫嘛！罗刹他也不想的。）
t_en: (He, uh... had no choice? Times are tough.)

t_ch: 不行。如果人人都能通融，规矩还有什么意义？
t_en: I can't do that. Why bother making rules if everyone can just ask for a favor?

t_ch: （看来这一招行不通啊……）
t_en: (Looks like this isn't gonna work...)

t_ch: （这普洱，做事还挺有原则。）
t_en: (Didn't take Pu'er for a man of rules.)

t_ch: （那毕竟是以杨叔为原型塑造的角色。）
t_en: (Don't forget — he's modeled after you, Mr. Yang.)

t_ch: 「执事精神」
t_en: Pretend to be an officer

t_ch: 对不起，我是地衡司的卧底。
t_en: My apologies. Truth is, I work undercover for the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 我有非常重要的公事，请立刻让你的老板来见我。
t_en: I have important matters to discuss with your boss. You need to bring him to me now.

t_ch: 您一身化外服装，和我说您是地衡司的人？糊弄谁呢？
t_en: Working undercover dressed like an outworlder? You think I'm stupid?

t_ch: 他说得好有道理…
t_en: He has a point...

t_ch: 你设计的反派居然有智商…
t_en: March, why do your characters act smarter than you do...

t_ch: （小三月在细节上会突然变得很有逻辑。）
t_en: (March's logic does seem to improve when it comes to minor details.)

t_ch: （我一直都很有逻辑！根据推演，假扮执事这一招是行不通的。）
t_en: (I'm always logical! And based on my deduction, pretending to be an officer isn't gonna work...)

t_ch: 「武力威胁」
t_en: Intimidating

t_ch: 我要见你们老板。如果你不愿意让开，我只好打到你愿意为止。
t_en: I said I need to see your boss. If you're not willing to cooperate, then perhaps some physical persuasion might be in order.

t_ch: （这个罗刹的人设也太多变了……）
t_en: (Luocha's personality changes by the minute...)

t_ch: 您威胁不了我，因为我有坚定的信念，绝不畏惧于强权。
t_en: Threats will get you nowhere. I have principles, and I don't give in to bullies.

t_ch: 这瓦尔特倒是很瓦尔特…
t_en: Strong Mr. Yang vibes...

t_ch: 这瓦尔特也不怎么瓦尔特…
t_en: Still a little aggressive for Mr. Yang...

t_ch: （这是普洱，不是杨叔！）
t_en: (Hey, this is Pu'er, not Mr. Yang!)

t_ch: 多说无益，看剑！
t_en: Enough talk. Have at you!

t_ch: （请先暂停一下，先别打。小三月，我认为他们应该没有打架…不然怎么进行交易呢。）
t_en: (Hang fire, March — I don't think Luocha was here to beat people up... He wanted to make a deal, right?)

t_ch: （杨叔说得对…那看来这一招行不通啊……）
t_en: (*sigh* True. Looks like using force isn't the right way...)

t_ch: 太可惜了，我还想看打架呢…
t_en: More's the pity — I wanted to see some action...

t_ch: 你就是看热闹不嫌事儿大是吧？
t_en: You're just here for the show!

t_ch: 那当然行不通了…
t_en: Of course not...

t_ch: 这叫「穷举推理」！渔公的拿手绝活之一！
t_en: Hey, this is called "proof by exhaustion!" It's what the Angler does best!

t_ch: 「破财消灾」
t_en: Bribing

t_ch: 这是我的全部积蓄，一共九千六百七十二万信用点。我把钱都给你，请你让我见见你的老板。
t_en: Here, this is all I have: <unbreak>96,720,000</unbreak> credits. Take it, and bring me to your boss.

t_ch: 赔钱做交易？
t_en: You think he'd spend his life savings on a bribe?

t_ch: （他们这些大反派，往往都有比金钱更加重要的目的。所以赔钱做生意，也是非常合理，非常符合逻辑的。）
t_en: (You know how evildoers can be — sometimes they have a higher purpose than just money. It's only natural he'd try everything to achieve that purpose.)

t_ch: 你信用点太多可以选择请客。
t_en: If you ever feel like you have too many credits, you could always take us out for dinner.... Just a thought.

t_ch: （说好让杨叔请客的，我可不能越俎代庖。嘿嘿。）
t_en: (I think it was Mr. Yang who promised us Xianzhou delicacies earlier? Wouldn't wanna steal his thunder, hee-hee...)

t_ch: 那可不行。私自收客人的钱，这要是被老板知道，我就死定了。
t_en: No can do. If the boss found out that I took a bribe, I'd be in big trouble.

t_ch: （嗯…看来这招行不通啊。）
t_en: (Hmm... Seems bribing isn't an option.)

t_ch: （小三月在这些无关紧要的细节上真的好有逻辑性。）
t_en: (When it comes to minor details, March's logic is actually pretty good.)

t_ch: （这哪里无关紧要了？这可是至关重要的推理呀。）
t_en: (How is this minor? It's an important link in my deduction!)

t_ch: 求求你了，求求你了，就让我见见你的老板吧！
t_en: Please let me see your boss? Pleaaaase?

t_ch: 看在我这么求你的份上，就通融一下吧，求求你了！
t_en: Have a heart, good sir! I'm begging you!

t_ch: 每天求我的人可多了，我难道都要通融一下？
t_en: People ask for favors every day. Am I supposed to say yes to all of them?

t_ch: （看来这招是行不通的。）
t_en: (This isn't working.)

t_ch: 行得通才怪。
t_en: Surprised?

t_ch: （不试试怎么知道！）
t_en: (Hey! You don't know if you don't try!)

t_ch: 罗刹真的是这种形象吗…
t_en: Is that... something Luocha would say?

t_ch: （我印象里的「他」，应该是从来不会求人的。）
t_en: (Doesn't sound like any of the "Luochas" I came across...)

t_ch: 「书籍扉页」
t_en: Title page

t_ch: 这是…接头暗号。看来真的是您。您稍等，我这就去把老大叫来。
t_en: That's... the code. So you're the one. Wait here a moment — I'll call the boss right away.

t_ch: 慢慢来，我不急。
t_en: Take your time.

t_ch: （普洱拿到接头用的扉页，就去通知他的老板了。过了不久，罗刹看到一个威严的身影向自己走来。）
t_en: (Pu'er takes the page and goes to find the boss. Before long, Luocha sees an imposing figure walking towards him.)

t_ch: 可恶帕，给我记住帕！
t_en: Darn it! This isn't over!

t_ch: 在长乐天闹得这么大，云骑很快就会出现…快点离开这里吧。
t_en: Fights like these don't go unnoticed in Exalting Sanctum — Cloud Knights could show up any minute... Better get going.

t_ch: （这样一来，罗刹就该跑路了，该跑到哪里去呢？我看看地图，喔，知道了！）
t_en: (And so Luocha has to make a break for it! But where to? Lemme have a look at the map... Aha, gotcha!)

t_ch: 云骑听到骚动，一定很快就会赶来，还是赶紧去码头吧。
t_en: The Cloud Knights will have been alerted by now — I need to hurry to the dock.

t_ch: 什么人在那里？不许动！
t_en: Halt! Who goes there?

t_ch: （在这种情况下，罗刹唯一能够逃生的路线就是……）
t_en: (Uh-oh! His only escape is...)

t_ch: 罗刹，快上来，该走了！
t_en: Climb aboard, Luocha! Time to get out of here!

t_ch: （就这样，罗刹跳上了星槎，在不明人士的帮助下逃离了现场。而这，这就是本次「罗刹消失事件」的全貌。）
t_en: (So Luocha hopped on a starskiff and fled with the help of his accomplice... And that, ladies and gents, is the truth behind Luocha's disappearance.)

t_ch: 开星槎来接罗刹的那位是？
t_en: So... who was piloting the starskiff?

t_ch: 我也不知道…反正不露脸，就当是{NICKNAME}吧。
t_en: I'm not sure either — the pilot didn't show themself, so... Let's just assume it was {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我怎么躺着也中枪…
t_en: How come I got dragged into this...

t_ch: 我就是希望大家都能登场露个脸嘛！
t_en: No one gets left out of my stories!

t_ch: 好歹给个露脸的角色啊！
t_en: At least let the audience see my face!

t_ch: 没办法啦，角色有限，下次吧。
t_en: Too many faces, too small a budget. Maybe next time.

t_ch: 小三月，虽然我不想打击你，但是…你的这段推理还是有一些小问题的。
t_en: ...March, a—as much as I enjoyed your deduction, I do have a few... questions.

t_ch: 比如，如果罗刹是乘坐星槎从码头逃走的，又怎么会在两小时后出现在机巧鸟的影像里？
t_en: For example, if Luocha took flight from the dock, how could he appear in cycrane footage two hours later?

t_ch: 那是个军用设施，稍有骚动，云骑就会赶来。但为什么会有怪物？
t_en: Also, the dock is a military installation — the Cloud Knights would be on the scene at the slightest disturbance. How did so many monsters show up without warning?

t_ch: 还有，即使按照这个千难万险的流程，罗刹也不会在那个地方滞留两小时。
t_en: Last but not least, even if Luocha did encounter all the troubles you described, it wouldn't have taken him two hours to escape.

t_ch: 嗯…杨叔说得很有道理，确实是这样哦！看来我神探三月七和渔公还有一点点小差距……
t_en: Hmm... You're right, Mr. Yang. Guess I can't compete with the Angler just yet...

t_ch: 这样吧，我们还是去现场看看吧，也许到了现场，很多谜题就迎刃而解了。
t_en: You know what? Time for some fieldwork. Maybe the answers to our questions are waiting for us at the scene.

t_ch: 嗯，这当然是最好的。我们走吧。
t_en: That might be the best approach. Let's go.

t_ch: 二位刚才说，被一个金发的化外民救了…此人可是这位罗刹？
t_en: Excuse me, gentlemen. Would this blonde-haired, life-saving outworlder bear any resemblance to our suspect, here?

t_ch: 没错，就是他。
t_en: That's him!

t_ch: 就是这位罗刹先生救了我们。
t_en: Mr. Luocha! He rescued both of us.

t_ch: 请问这是什么时候发生的事呢？
t_en: Can I ask when this rescue occurred?

t_ch: 应该是在…建木生发的前一天。
t_en: It was... the day before the Ambrosial Arbor came back to life.

t_ch: 第二天就出了那么大的事，我们也一直没来得及找他说一声谢谢。
t_en: We were planning to thank him properly, but in the aftermath of the Arbor incident, we never had the time.

t_ch: 难道你们就是…「黑衣人」？你们怎么不穿黑衣了啊？
t_en: Are you two... *gasp* the people in dark clothing? What's with the wardrobe change?

t_ch: 什么「黑衣人」？…哦，我明白了。您可别提那倒霉「黑衣人」了。哪儿来的什么「黑衣人」。
t_en: Dark clothing? ...Ah! I know what you're talking about. What a pair of clowns we are... Dark clothing, hmph.

t_ch: 那是我们俩掉沟里了。
t_en: We... fell into a ditch.

t_ch: 嗯…嗯？什么情况？
t_en: Wait, what?

t_ch: 他给我们哥俩的就是这个东西。
t_en: Here, that's the prescription he gave us.

t_ch: 纸花？叠得真好看。这是罗刹叠的？
t_en: A paper flower? It's beautiful... Did Luocha make this?

t_ch: 对，他将药方写在上面，叠成了一朵纸花。
t_en: That's right, he wrote down the prescription and folded it into shape.

t_ch: 已经很久没见过这么浪漫的人了。
t_en: A man of romantic sensibilities, one might say.

t_ch: 但是这张纸…有点眼熟……我能拆开看看吗？
t_en: The paper looks familiar... can I open it?

t_ch: 当然可以，我们抓药时已经拆开过了。
t_en: Of course. We'd already opened it when we showed it to the pharmacy.

t_ch: 我们本就打算将这个东西交给地衡司，也算是寻找恩公的线索。
t_en: We were planning on handing it over to the Realm-Keeping Commission — we suspect it's probably our best chance of tracing him.

t_ch: 交给我们吧，有消息后会联系二位的。
t_en: We can take it off you — we'll let you know when we find him.

t_ch: 我看看…啊，果然！
t_en: Let me see... Ah! Of course!

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 这是《渔公案》的扉页！
t_en: It's the title page of The Angler Mystery!

t_ch: 我看看…这一面是药方，这一面好像也有字…啊！原来是这样啊！
t_en: The prescription's on this side, but on the back there's... Huh? So that's what this is all about!?

t_ch: 背面写了什么？
t_en: What's on the back?

t_ch: 背面是接头暗号？
t_en: Is it a secret code?

t_ch: 很可惜，不是这样啦。
t_en: Sadly not...

t_ch: 你自己看吧。
t_en: Take a look yourself.

t_ch: 什么「剧透仙人」啊，单纯就是没素质！
t_en: "The Immortal Spoiler"? Ugh, some people just wanna watch the world burn.

t_ch: 看来几位相中的罗刹先生，非但不是坏人，甚至是个古道热肠的好人啊。
t_en: I'm starting to think the Luocha we envisioned doesn't square with the real one... Would a villain do a good deed like this?

t_ch: 为了防止别人被这个可恶的「剧透仙人」伤害，罗刹撕下了被写下剧透内容的扉页……
t_en: So he paid for the book and tore off the title page to protect people from the spoiler...

t_ch: 会做出这样的举动，说明他真的很喜欢《渔公案》。而喜欢这本书的人、喜欢渔公这位神探的人，又怎么会去做坏事呢？
t_en: He must be a fan of this book too... I knew Angler Mystery fans couldn't be bad!

t_ch: 看来还是我们想错啦。
t_en: Looks like the whole thing was just a big misunderstanding.

t_ch: 是啊，以貌取人，总是不好的…算了，不谈这些了。咱们走吧。
t_en: Indeed, and I've learned not to judge a book by its cover... Anyway, that's enough reflection for one day. Let's get going.

t_ch: 杨叔，我们要去哪儿啊？
t_en: Get going to where, Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 至味盛苑。请你们俩吃好吃的。
t_en: Garden of Gourmet. You two deserve a treat.

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yay!

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yay!

t_ch: 不要紧…我会解决的。
t_en: Worry not... I'll handle it.

t_ch: 是啊，这一切与你我无关…旅程才刚开始……
t_en: Yes. None of this is our concern... The journey has only just begun to—

t_ch: 嗯？怎么突然没声音了！
t_en: Huh? Where'd the sound go!?

t_ch: 怎么这样啊…听人说话只说一半，就像照片只能洗出半张一样难受……
t_en: Ugh, don't do this to me... Hearing half a sentence is like developing half a photo. I'll be up all night thinking about it...

t_ch: （即便罗刹可以解开门锁，他进去的目的是什么呢？）
t_en: (Even if Luocha was able to unlock the door, what was his goal?)

t_ch: （和他交接星核的坏家伙藏在这里，他就是来和那些家伙见面的。）
t_en: (His villain friends must have been waiting on the other side to buy the Stellaron. He went to meet them.)

t_ch: （想了想，不太对吧？他们怎么会在一个军机重地见面？）
t_en: (Now that I think about it, something's not right. Why would they rendezvous at a military airfield?)

t_ch: （啊…这…这是因为，我们平时遵纪守法，很难理解坏家伙们的逻辑。）
t_en: (B—Because, uhh... Well, we're too law-abiding to understand bad guy logic.)

t_ch: （…越危险的地方越安全是吗。）
t_en: (...Hiding in plain sight, perhaps.)

t_ch: 买家在那里等我。过去看看情况吧。
t_en: The buyer is waiting there, time to head over.

t_ch: （嗯，从地图上来看，应该只有这里最为隐蔽。）
t_en: (Plausible — looking at the map, this place is well-hidden.)

t_ch: （天哪，我神探三月七终于获得了杨叔的认可……）
t_en: (Finally, Mr. Yang approves detective March's theory!)

t_ch: （…继续吧。）
t_en: (...Let's see where this leads first.)

t_ch: 您好，这位执事，我们想找一个人。
t_en: Excuse me, officer — we're looking for someone. Can you help us?

t_ch: 应该是个化外民，金发，打扮不像仙舟人士。
t_en: He's got blonde hair, and judging by the way he's dressed, I'd say he was an outworlder.

t_ch: 金发的异邦人？该不会是…二位找他什么事？
t_en: A traveler with blonde hair? Could it be... May I ask why you're looking for him?

t_ch: 此人救了我们一命。
t_en: He saved our life.

t_ch: 其实是两命。
t_en: Since when did we start sharing a life?

t_ch: 阿来，这种时候就不要捧哏了。
t_en: Back'n, we're not rehearsing...

t_ch: 对不住，习惯了。
t_en: Sorry, old habits.

t_ch: （你这是要去哪儿？不是要推理罗刹的去向吗？怎么往回走了？）
t_en: (Where are you going? We're trying to trace Luocha's movements — we shouldn't backtrack.)

t_ch: （他们应该不会在码头上接头…这里太开阔了，感觉很不适合邪恶计划……）
t_en: (They wouldn't have met up at the dock, it's too open. No good for discussing evil plans...)

t_ch: 我们想找一个人。
t_en: We're looking for someone.

t_ch: 打扮不像仙舟人。
t_en: I'd say he was an outworlder.

t_ch: 二位找他什么事？
t_en: May I ask why you're looking for him?

t_ch: 此人救了我们一命。
t_en: He saved our life.

t_ch: 其实是两命。
t_en: Since when did we start sharing a life?

t_ch: 这种时候别捧哏。
t_en: We're not rehearsing...

t_ch: 对不住，习惯了。
t_en: Sorry, old habits.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，远处似乎能看到有地方在冒烟。这是夕葵小姐派来的机巧鸟，用它飞上去看看吧。情况紧急，我们得抓紧时间确认坠机位置。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, looks like there's smoke rising in the distance. Miss Xikui sent us a cycrane — see if you can get an aerial visual. This is an emergency, we need to find where the starskiff crashed as soon as possible.

t_ch: 现在不是闲聊的时候。救人要紧。
t_en: I'm afraid there's no time for chit-chat — lives could be at stake.

t_ch: 找到失事地点了…看来飞行士并不在里面。太好了。如果是在失事前逃了出去，那现在人应该还在附近。
t_en: We found it... looks like the pilot isn't inside. If they escaped before it came down, they should still be nearby.

t_ch: 不妙，有些怪物被坠机的骚动惊扰了。情况容不得耽搁。
t_en: Not good — looks like the crash disturbed monsters in the area. We need to get there — fast.

t_ch: 找到了。那应该就是夕葵小姐拜托我们救的人。她被孽物困住了，咱们得加紧脚步。{NICKNAME}，你也要注意安全。
t_en: There — that must be who Miss Xikui asked us to save. We need to hurry — those abominations have trapped her. Be careful, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你怎么一个人就跑过来了？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, why are you here on your own?

t_ch: 我知道你救人心切，但只有查清现场的情况，才方便救援。时间耽搁不起了，咱们动身吧。
t_en: I know you want to help, but we need more intel on the crash site before we start the rescue. There's no time to lose — let's go.

t_ch: 妖物，别靠近我！我、我不怕你们！你不要过来啊！
t_en: Get back, you demons! I—I'm not afraid of you! Stay away!

t_ch: 喂！这里很危险！你们快走，快去天舶司叫人来救我，就说晴霓遇到危险了！
t_en: Hey! Get out of here — it's too dangerous! Go get someone from the Sky-Faring Commission — tell them Qingni is in trouble!

t_ch: 别担心，我们很强。
t_en: It's okay, we're pretty tough.

t_ch: 晴霓小姐不必担心，我们经历过比这更危险的情况。
t_en: Don't worry, Miss Qingni. We've been in worse danger.

t_ch: 区区孽物，何谈危险？
t_en: They're only abominations — we got this.

t_ch: 晴霓小姐不必担心，我们经历过比这更危险的情况。
t_en: Don't worry, Miss Qingni. We've been in worse danger.

t_ch: 那好，一会儿见。
t_en: Okay, see you later then.

t_ch: 不，{NICKNAME}，现在搬救兵肯定来不及了。晴霓小姐不必担心，正是天舶司的人派我们前来救急的。
t_en: No, {NICKNAME}, it's too late to call for backup. Don't worry, Miss Qingni — the Sky-Faring Commission sent us to help.

t_ch: 肃清了附近的孽物之后，云骑将被困的少女救了下来。
t_en: After cleansing the area of the abomination, the Cloud Knights rescued the trapped girl.

t_ch: 没想到天舶司的司舵会亲赴现场处理这么危险的事。
t_en: I didn't know the helm master of the Sky-Faring Commission dealt with situations like this in person.

t_ch: 我本来应当坐镇司辰宫批阅公文。但晴霓以身犯险，贪功冒进，甚至要惊动贵客周护她的安全。我这个做母亲的怎么还能坐得住？
t_en: Indeed, I should be reviewing files in the Palace of Astrum. However, Qingni decided to put herself in danger and trouble our esteemed guests with her safety... As her mother, I couldn't sit idly by.

t_ch: 母亲…
t_en: Mother...

t_ch: 原来如此…
t_en: So that's why...

t_ch: 晴霓！还不向二位恩公道谢？
t_en: Qingni! Have you thanked your benefactors yet?

t_ch: 谢谢二位恩公……
t_en: Thank you, benefactors...

t_ch: 不，晴霓小姐不必多礼，只是举手之劳罢了。
t_en: No need, Miss Qingni. It was nothing.

t_ch: 这种事比扫垃圾还简单。
t_en: As easy as taking out the trash.

t_ch: 不愧是星穹列车上的开拓者啊，气度不凡！虽然比喻有些奇特……
t_en: You Astral Express trailblazers have outstanding manners, though your allegories are a little odd...

t_ch: 多亏驭空大人及时出现。
t_en: It was all thanks to Madam Yukong's timely appearance.

t_ch: 不敢当。这本就是天舶司分内之事，却让二位英雄以身犯险。我实在惭愧。
t_en: You flatter me. This is a Sky-Faring Commission affair, yet the two of you had to put yourselves in harm's way. I am ashamed.

t_ch: 我寻思我也没做什么…
t_en: I didn't really do anything...

t_ch: 不必谦虚。两位为仙舟以身犯险，这一切我都看在眼中。
t_en: No need to be humble, you took great risks for the Xianzhou. I saw what you did.

t_ch: 这一次多亏了二位英雄，晴霓才能安然无恙。我的感激之情难以言表。
t_en: It was because of both of you that Qingni escaped unscathed. Words cannot express my gratitude.

t_ch: 这样吧，接下来请二位来司辰宫稍坐，我们饮茶小叙一番…一是奉上香茗当做答谢，二是与两位英雄交个朋友。
t_en: In fact, by way of thanks, I'd like to invite you to the Palace of Astrum for tea. It is about time we knew each other better.

t_ch: 我们也想在仙舟多多增进了解，结交朋友。能有这样的机会，也是我们的荣幸。
t_en: The feeling is mutual, Lady Yukong. We would be honored.

t_ch: 那么，我们就先行告辞了。因为晴霓的胡闹，给二位带来了这么多麻烦，请容我再次向二位英雄道歉。
t_en: Then for now I will excuse myself. Qingni's irresponsible actions caused much trouble for the two of you — allow me to apologize once again on her behalf.

t_ch: 您又是这样，都不问事情的来龙去脉，就先骂我一顿……
t_en: You always do this, mother — scold me before figuring out what actually happened...

t_ch: 当时的情况很紧急！只有我有那个技术……
t_en: It was a dire situation! I was the only one who could—

t_ch: 但你答应过我的，晴霓！…这件事咱们回家再说。
t_en: You promised me, Qingni! ...We shall discuss this when we return.

t_ch: 抱歉，让二位见笑了。之后见。
t_en: Please excuse us, benefactors. I look forward to our meeting.

t_ch: 真没想到，驭空还有这样的一面……
t_en: I didn't know Yukong had that side to her...

t_ch: 你是说当妈的一面？
t_en: You mean a scary mom side?

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}，你观察的角度总是那么…独特。
t_en: ...You have a... unique way of putting things, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 确实，如果光看外表，谁也没法想象仙舟长生种活了几倍于我们的人生。
t_en: ...but you're not wrong. Appearances seemingly tell us little about the lives of the Xianzhou's leaders, no matter how long those lives may have been.

t_ch: 只有在面对晴霓小姐这样的亲人时，驭空表露出的烦恼和关心才会让人意识到她同时也是某人的母亲。
t_en: Yukong's inner worries and concerns came to the surface in the presence of her daughter. Only then was it clear that she, too, is somebody's mother.

t_ch: 感觉杨叔很能体会这种心情…
t_en: I feel like you relate to this a lot, Mr. Yang...

t_ch: 是啊，毕竟我也曾是个操心孩子课业、还有明天给他做些什么菜的老父亲。
t_en: Yes. After all, I know what it's like to worry over a kid's homework and whether their lunch is healthy enough.

t_ch: 你是说善战的一面？
t_en: You mean a warlike side?

t_ch: 是啊…我本以为天舶司的主事者是长袖善舞的商人，就像那个小狐女一样。
t_en: Yes... I originally thought the head of the Sky-Faring Commission was a suave businesswoman — like that young foxian girl...

t_ch: 但从刚才的战斗能看得出来，驭空是个经验老到的战士。她弯弓的时机，目标的选择，全都无可挑剔。
t_en: ...but based on that fight, Yukong is clearly an experienced soldier. The moments she chose to strike and the targets she attacked were all impeccably strategic.

t_ch: 司舵亲自上阵，除了救回女儿之外，恐怕也是找到了难得的机会一试身手。
t_en: Aside from saving her daughter, perhaps the Helm Master was keen to see some front-line action.

t_ch: 杨叔一副深有感触的样子。
t_en: Sounds like you understand the feeling, Mr. Yang.

t_ch: 让你笑话了。我曾经在故乡悠闲度日了许久，久到几乎忘了自己曾经战斗过。自从登上列车后，沉睡许久的热情也渐渐回来了。
t_en: Hehe, well... I enjoyed a long stretch of peace on my home world — long enough for me to start forgetting that I was also a fighter. After I boarded the Express, my old enthusiasm slowly began to return.

t_ch: 不过有件事我不太明白。晴霓虽然擅自开走星槎抢险，但毕竟有功劳在前。可驭空刚才对她的态度，却像是另有隐情……
t_en: But there's something I don't understand. Yes, Qingni piloted a starskiff without permission and put herself in danger, but she meant well. Nevertheless, Yukong's reaction suggests that there was something more...

t_ch: 不说这些，咱们还是早点到司辰宫比较好。别让驭空等我们。
t_en: Anyway, let's head over to the Palace of Astrum — best not to keep Yukong waiting.

t_ch: 也不必提前到吧…
t_en: We don't have to get there early, right...?

t_ch: 咱们是客人，客人有客人要守的规矩。
t_en: No, but we've been invited — we certainly don't want to be late.

t_ch: 出发！去司辰宫！
t_en: TO THE PALACE!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，进了司辰宫可不能突然这么拔高声线说话。
t_en: Uhh... Let's avoid sudden exclamations like that once we're in the Palace, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 咱们是去和天舶司的司舵喝茶，不是去朋友家玩。在外交场合，说话做事都要更加得体庄重些。
t_en: Remember, this is no field trip, we're having tea with the helm master of the Sky-Faring Commission. We'll need to act with diplomatic decorum.

t_ch: 好了，时间不早了，咱们出发吧。
t_en: Alright, let's head out before it gets late.

t_ch: 真不好意思，我们出现得不是时候。
t_en: Apologies, Madam Yukong, we should have given you some time.

t_ch: 真对不起…
t_en: Yes, apologies...

t_ch: 与两位无关，是我不分场合，失仪了。
t_en: On the contrary, I apologize for allowing my own personal matters to encroach on your time.

t_ch: 杨叔，都说了晚些再来…
t_en: Mr. Yang, I told you we'd be early...

t_ch: 的确，是我疏忽了。
t_en: You're right, an oversight on my part.

t_ch: 与两位无关，是我不分场合，失仪了。
t_en: On the contrary, I apologize for allowing my own personal matters to encroach on your time.

t_ch: 我们无意窥探驭空司舵的隐私。不过，如果您有什么苦恼我们能帮上忙的，还请不要客气。
t_en: We don't intend to pry into private business, Helm Master Yukong, but if there's anything we can help with, please don't hesitate to ask.

t_ch: 不过是些家事罢了，两位刚才应该听到只言片语了吧？
t_en: Family issues, I'm afraid. You probably heard a thing or two just now?

t_ch: 晴霓这孩子一心想成为斗舰飞行士，可我没有允许，我让她好好做一个文员。这可能显得我很专断，但是，一想起她被怪物困住那一幕，我就后怕得尾巴打战……
t_en: Despite my earnest wish that she continue in her post, Qingni is fixated on becoming a fighter pilot. I may seem overbearing, but the situations she gets herself into send a shiver up my tail...

t_ch: 说来可笑。丰饶联军也好，活化的星球也罢，什么样的绝境我都曾目睹过，本以为自己是个不知害怕为何物的人。
t_en: Hehe, it's funny, isn't it? I've seen every disaster imaginable, from the Abundance Axis to a living planet. I thought I'd forgotten what fear was.

t_ch: 和你们一同战斗时，我才明白，原来这世上真有能让我感到恐惧的事…抱歉，谈这些糟心事打扰了客人的雅兴。
t_en: But when I fought alongside you, I realized there are still things that can terrify me... Sorry. Talking about such vexing matters is unbecoming in front of guests.

t_ch: 今日请来二位，是为了加深与列车的了解，切莫让这些琐事扫了兴致。
t_en: I invited you here to gain a better understanding of the Express, not bore you with these trivialities.

t_ch: 作为赔罪，我为二位准备了茶点，请来品品这上好的鳞渊春！
t_en: Oh, I prepared refreshments for you. Please, you must try this excellent Whale-Tide Spring!

t_ch: 虽然遭遇了尴尬的开场，但这番会面你们与驭空倒也谈的尽兴。
t_en: Though things didn't get off to a great start, everyone enjoyed this meeting with Yukong.

t_ch: 能和遍行星海的无名客谈天，真是受益匪浅。这些礼物，是为感谢你们救下小女准备的，请不要推辞。
t_en: Please accept these gifts as thanks for saving my daughter. I'd heard that the Nameless had traversed the starry sea, but I wasn't prepared for how much I could learn from you.

t_ch: 我听天舶司的人说起过，驭空大人是罗浮数一数二的飞行士，云骑军中的王牌，上一回瞧见您挽弓的模样，便知道传言不假。希望未来能再有幸目睹您驾驭星槎的神技。
t_en: Well, I'd heard from members of the Sky-Faring Commission that you were a top-tier pilot, Madam Yukong, and an ace among the Cloud Knights. Now that I've seen you in action, I know the rumors were true. I hope to witness your flying ability for myself one day.

t_ch: 司舵公务繁忙，我们也不便叨扰。希望以后还有机会和您多多交流。
t_en: We'll head off now — the Commission must be busy. I look forward to our next meeting, Madam Yukong.

t_ch: 恐怕要让瓦尔特先生失望了。天空，已经不再适合我了。
t_en: On my flying ability, I'm afraid I'll have to disappoint you, Mr. Yang. The sky no longer suits me.

t_ch: 看来{NICKNAME}谈兴不减。
t_en: Looks like you still wish to talk, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 似乎每次与你们见面，都伴随着意外。希望下次能在更合适的气氛下与星穹列车的各位见面。
t_en: It seems our every meeting is accompanied by an incident of some sort. I hope I can meet the Astral Express crew under better circumstances next time.

t_ch: 二位！请留步！
t_en: You two! Please wait!

t_ch: 本来应该再次感谢二位恩公的救命之恩…但这一回又让你们看了笑话，真是非常抱歉……
t_en: Thank you for saving me, benefactors... and I'm sorry you had to witness all that...

t_ch: 亲子关系嘛…理解，理解。
t_en: I understand. It's the parent-child relationship.

t_ch: 看来恩公也遇到过类似的事情吗？真是太好了！不是不是，我的意思是，终于给我遇上了一个有共鸣的人。
t_en: So you've experienced it yourself, benefactor? That's great! I—I mean... I'm happy I finally found someone who can understand me.

t_ch: 你不该那么和母亲讲话。
t_en: You shouldn't have talked like that to your mother.

t_ch: 恩公也这么说吗…我、我当然知道这样激烈地表达只会带来反效果。
t_en: You think so too, benefactor...? I—I mean, I know getting emotional only makes things worse.

t_ch: 我已经过了及笄礼，是个成年人了，我有实现梦想的自由吧！可是妈妈却还是把我当个孩子看，把我笼在她的翅膀下。好像离开了她，我就一刻也活不下去一样。
t_en: But I've had the Hairpin Ceremony — I'm an adult now! I have the right to pursue my dreams! Mom still insists on sheltering me under her wing, as if I can't even stay alive without her.

t_ch: 咳，晴霓小姐，这是你的家务事，我不便置喙。但是，我可以说些亲身体验——
t_en: Ahem... Miss Qingni, these are family matters, I'm not sure it's my place to comment. However, in my personal experience...

t_ch: 为人父母是会让一个人变得顽固的。要改变岩石，恐怕还得水滴日复一日地耐心琢磨。
t_en: ...becoming a parent often means we become obstinate. To erode a rock, one must be patient and persistent, like water.

t_ch: 所以，晴霓小姐与其像今天这样爆发，不如耐心用行动来证明，自己可以照顾好自己？
t_en: What I mean to say is, instead of trying to prove yourself suddenly, perhaps you could demonstrate your ability to look after yourself methodically, over time. Don't you think, Miss Qingni?
